name: Dmitri Shostakovich
abstract: Dmitri Dmitriyevich Shostakovich (25 September [O.S. 12 September] 1906 – 9 August 1975) was a Soviet-era Russian composer and pianist who became internationally known after the premiere of his First Symphony in 1926 and thereafter was regarded as a major composer.

about_quotes.speaker: Longineu Parsons
about_quotes.evidence: Since I was a kid, his music has spoken to me, and now as a quote unquote scholar or whatever I am, then I really appreciate his sense of motific development.
about_quotes.doc_title: Longineu Parsons interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 17, 2026

about_quotes.speaker: Skitch Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: Schoenberg said, 'I did fifty of those, none of them ever became famous.' He was a strange, strange man. I was frightened to death of him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Skitch Henderson interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Milford, Connecticut, October 31, 1998
